article_meta.title: SCOTUS Pro-Life Center Takes Its Fight Against Democrat Lawfare To The Supreme Court By: Shawn Fleetwood November 21, 20

reasoning: Some genuine author-prose bias was identified, but there were attribution violations (quoted source language flagged), widespread use of non-approved bias type labels, and a few missed or under-identified instances. Output also included markdown fences.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged quoted statements from sources (e.g., Huber’s and Wilson’s quotes) as author bias, violating attribution rules.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items reflected genuine author-level slant/word choice or structural omission; however, two items were invalid due to being quotes.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed some clear author-prose spin in the lede (e.g., 'gearing up to fight back') and additional loaded labels, though several key instances were caught.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used non-listed categories (Loaded Language, Framing Bias, Source Bias, Labeling Bias) instead of approved types like Word Choice, Slant, Bias by Omission, etc.
